course_description.overview: Endurance road race on the 5.8 km Suzuka Circuit, a technical F1 track with elevation changes, high-speed sections, and challenging corners like the 'S' curves and 130R corner. Teams and soloists complete laps for a set time period.

key_segments.name: Degner Curves
key_segments.description: Tight left-right sequence testing bike handling and rider skill.

key_segments.name: Spoon Curve
key_segments.description: Long uphill left-hander requiring sustained power.

key_segments.name: 130R
key_segments.description: High-speed right-hander at over 300 km/h demanding precision.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: High due to relentless pace, technical corners, and team coordination over hours; fatigue and strategy key factors.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Chubu Centrair International Airport (NGO) or Kansai International (KIX), then train or bus to Suzuka (1-2 hours).
logistics.accommodation: On-site hotel at Suzuka Circuit, nearby hotels in Suzuka city, or camping areas.
logistics.registration_details: Entry via official Suzuka Circuit website; team-based with seed rights for top teams; strict bike class rules (e.g., 600cc supersport).
logistics.bike_logistics: Bikes transported to circuit; pit areas provided for teams; modifications limited per class rules.
logistics.local_considerations: Hot, humid summer conditions; large crowds (up to 85,000+); Japanese language dominant, English support limited.

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic Japanese motorcycle endurance racing on legendary Suzuka Circuit.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced road racers, teams, and fans of high-level circuit endurance events.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes for prestige and competition, but high costs and team requirements limit accessibility.
pro_tips: Prepare for heat and humidity in July.
Secure team entry early via seed rights or qualifiers.
Study Suzuka's technical layout for lap time optimization.
Leverage pit strategy for refueling and rider changes.
